figures.caption: Fig. 3. Qualitative comparison on challenging Night Photography Rendering scenes.

Fig. 2. The pHVI-ISPNet Architecture. Our network extends the base CIDNet [2] dual-branch structure with two key structural innovations designed for NPR (i.e., the RAW-domain feature processing block and Wavelet-based feature propagation).
Table 1. Quantitative Comparison on Night Photography Rendering benchmark [1].

Fig. 1. Perceptual gap in challenging night-time scenes. While SOTA method (a) achieves high PSNR/SSIM, it suffers from severe perceptual distortion (e.g., color bias, tone artifacts) relative to GT (c). Our approach (b) delivers superior perceptual realism (i.e., faithful color, robust detail).
